REPLY BRIEF IN SUPPORT OF PLTF'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF CHAN, LIBORIO

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFFS JUAN CARLOS ARANA MORENO, JULIAN POOT, LIBORIO CHAN, LUIS ALBERTO COLLI PERERA, EDGAR DIAZ, JOSE MENCIA, RAUL ENRIQUE MISEK, SAMIR ROQUE, AND JUAN ANTONIO SALDIVCAR'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS GRANTED. THE COURT HAS JURISDICTION OVER THIS CASE AFTER THE DISTRICT COURT ORDERED THE CASE REMANDED TO STATE COURT. THERE IS A LIBERAL POLICY IN FAVOR OF AMENDMENT, THE AMENDMENTS DO NOT CHANGE THE TENOR OF THE CASE, AND THE AMENDMENTS WILL NOT PREJUDICE DEFENDANT. THE COURT DENIES DEFENDANT'S REQUEST TO INDEFINITELY DEFER RULING ON THIS MOTION. DEFENDANT COULD HAVE SOUGHT TO STAY THE DISTRICT COURT'S ORDER OF REMAND IN FEDERAL COURT. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER #5976.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-29-2014 Set For Trial On Jun-29-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion Foar Leave To File First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jun-04-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Karen Carrera, Esq., and Felicia Goldstein, Esq., 415.989.8000, for plaintiffs Juan Carlos Arana Moreno, Julian Poot, Liborio Chan, Luis Alberto Colli Perera, Edgar Diaz, Jose Mencia, Raul Enrique Misek, Samir Roque and Juan Antonio Saldivcar.
S. Shane Sagheb, Esq., 310.557.9572, for defendants Crab Addison, Inc. and Ignite Restaurant Group, Inc.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: PLAINTIFFS JUAN CARLOS ARANA MORENO, JULIAN POOT, LIBORIO CHAN, LUIS ALBERTO COLLI PERERA, EDGAR DIAZ, JOSE MENCIA, RAUL ENRIQUE MISEK, SAMIR ROQUE, and JUAN ANTONIO SALDIVCAR'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint is granted. The court has jurisdiction over this case after the district court ordered the case remanded to state court. There is a liberal policy in favor of amendment, the amendments do not change the tenor of the case, and the amendments will not prejudice defendant. The court denies defendant's request to indefinitely defer ruling on this motion. Defendant could have sought to stay the district court's order of remand in federal court. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer #5976.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-06-2014 Off Calendar
